l10n.text: How quickly mouse movement translates to steering angle. Low = smooth, high = snappy.
Steering output below this value is clamped to zero. Prevents front wheel jitter.
Mouse movement below this value is ignored. Useful for high-DPI mice.
Top-of-screen bar showing the current steering angle while LMB steering or during the short return-to-centre after you release LMB.

When the green ground lines are visible. Off = never, On steering = whenever the wheels are turned (mouse, keyboard, wheel, controller), Mouse only = during LMB mouse steering and the short coast after release, Always = while in any vehicle.
When reversing with a trailer attached, project a yellow line showing where the trailer's rear edges will swing. Uses the trailer's current hitch angle and the vehicle steering arc — updates live.

While holding LMB to steer, yaw the cabin camera slightly with steering so the view follows the bend (like looking through a corner on a bike). Mouse look stays suppressed for LMB-only steering; use RMB+LMB for free-look.

Extra camera yaw at full steering (degrees each side). A natural “in the bend” glance is often ~25–40°. For a proper look at cross traffic (like a shoulder check), try ~70–100°; ~90° is a strong sideways view.
How quickly the camera follows steering and returns when you release. Higher = snappier.
Flip left/right if the camera turns the wrong way for your vehicle or camera mod.
After releasing LMB, try to read the same steering return / centre speed as in Options (when the game exposes it). If off, only the value below is used.

How fast mouse steering eases back to centre after LMB release. Higher = faster (aligned with in-game steering return). Used when game read is disabled or unavailable. Scaling is tuned to feel close to keyboard return speed.
